mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns during rate hike periods with statistical significance (p < 0.05), or rolling Rank IC exceeds 0.05 positively.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals an imminent recessionary trough, prompting market participants to price in future monetary easing and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain negative or flat when unemployment crosses 4.0 percent, or the signal fails to produce a positive mean return in backtests spanning 2000-2022.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales growth and consumer price inflation indicates overheating aggregate demand that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of historical occurrences, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction that precedes inventory destocking and eventual demand stabilization, triggering positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during tightening cycles consistently coincide with negative 5-day forward returns, or the predictive power vanishes when filtering for tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with a rising federal funds rate creates a stagflationary pressure environment that erodes real corporate earnings and equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a positive or zero coefficient in out-of-sample tests, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower when both series trend upward simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistical correlation with 60-day forward returns, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in housing starts below 1.4 million units signals collapsing construction demand and broader credit tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts falling below 1.4 million units consistently precede positive 5-day forward returns, or the threshold signal fails to achieve a win rate above 50 percent in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production following a supply chain disruption signals restored manufacturing throughput that improves corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production rebounds during recovery phases consistently coincide with negative 20-day forward returns, or the signal loses predictive power when isolated to post-shock periods.
